title: Optimize images and fonts on the load path
description: Right-size, format, and lazy-load images and control font loading so media does not dominate LCP or cause layout shift.
body: Steps: 1. Identify the LCP element and the largest media on the route. 2. Serve responsive sizes in a modern format (WebP/AVIF), set width/height or aspect-ratio to reserve space, and lazy-load offscreen images. 3. Preload the LCP image; avoid lazy-loading it. 4. Set font-display swap/optional and preload critical fonts to avoid invisible-text and reflow. 5. Re-measure. Rollback point: before changing the LCP image strategy. Validation: the network panel shows reduced bytes, CLS stays within budget because dimensions are reserved, and LCP improves in a trace; a smaller file with worse layout shift is not a win.
